metaTitle: Paste a Table from Excel or Word Directly | Tablesmit
metaDescription: Copy a table from Excel, Word, or Google Sheets and paste into Tablesmit. It generates from your clipboard automatically. No file upload, no import dialog.
heroHeadline: Copy from Excel. Paste into Tablesmit. Done.
heroSubtext: Ctrl+C in Excel, Ctrl+V in Tablesmit. Your table appears in full — headers, rows, and data exactly as copied. No import dialog, no column mapping, no cleanup.

benefits.heading: Instant — no import dialog
benefits.body: Most tools show you a multi-step import wizard when you paste table data. Tablesmit reads the clipboard directly and generates the table in one keypress.

benefits.heading: Detects the format automatically
benefits.body: Excel copies tab-separated values. Word copies HTML table markup. CSV data uses commas. Tablesmit detects all three and parses correctly — you don't choose the format.

benefits.heading: Full structure preserved
benefits.body: Rows, columns, and cell values are mapped correctly. Copy a 10-column, 30-row spreadsheet range and all 300 cells appear in the right positions.

benefits.heading: Never leaves your browser
benefits.body: The clipboard is read entirely locally using the Web Clipboard API. Nothing is sent to a server. Your data stays in your browser.

steps.heading: Select and copy your table
steps.body: In Excel, Google Sheets, or Word — select the cells or table you want. Press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on Mac).

steps.heading: Open Tablesmit and paste
steps.body: Open Tablesmit at tablesmit.com. Press Ctrl+V anywhere on the page. The table generates immediately.

steps.heading: Style and export
steps.body: Your table is now fully editable in Tablesmit. Apply a theme, resize columns, merge cells, add a caption — then export to PDF, LaTeX, Excel, or any format you need.

useCases: Copying a dataset from Excel into Tablesmit to style it and export as a PDF report
Pasting a results table from Google Sheets into Tablesmit to export as LaTeX for a journal paper
Taking a Word table into Tablesmit to apply proper column types and re-export as clean Excel
Moving a Notion database view into Tablesmit for custom header colours and PDF export

_codex_notes.note: sources[] is an additional array. Render as a compact compatibility table between the hero and benefits: Source | Format | Works (✓/✗). Show the 'works: false' row for charts/images to set honest expectations.
